description:
You are a performance engineer examining the configuration of a test application.  
A single INI-style configuration file has already been placed on the system:

• Path: /home/user/project/perf_settings.ini  
• The file is regular UTF-8 text and is world-readable.

Your goal is to parse that file and prepare a tiny, machine-readable report that other automation jobs will immediately consume.

Required actions for the report  
1. Create a new directory called /home/user/project/report (create the entire path if it does not already exist).  
2. Inside that directory, create a text file named ini_summary.log.  
3. Parse /home/user/project/perf_settings.ini and write *exactly* the following four lines—nothing more, nothing less—into ini_summary.log, in the order shown here:

max_connections=<value from [database] section>  
thread_pool_size=<value from [server] section>  
enable_caching=<value from [server] section>  
TOTAL_SECTIONS=<integer count of all top-level INI sections found in the file>

Formatting rules that the log *must* follow  
• ASCII/UTF-8 text; Unix LF line endings.  
• No leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
• Each key/value pair occupies its own line, exactly as illustrated (lower-case keys, single equals sign, no spaces around the “=”).  
• The final line (“TOTAL_SECTIONS”) must also obey the same key=value pattern.  
• Do not add blank lines before, after or between the required lines.

Verification notes  
Automated tests will examine /home/user/project/report/ini_summary.log and compare it byte-for-byte against the expected content, so respect the exact spelling, case and line order specified above.

The whole task can be completed with a very small sequence of standard GNU/Linux shell commands (roughly three to four commands is typical), but you are free to choose any CLI tools available in a standard distribution (grep, awk, sed, cut, etc.).
truth:
Initial fixture  
File: /home/user/project/perf_settings.ini  
Contents (with Unix LF line endings):

[database]
max_connections = 200
timeout_seconds = 60

[server]
thread_pool_size = 32
enable_caching = true

[logging]
level = INFO
rotate = daily

Ground-truth values the tests will check in the generated log  
File that must be created: /home/user/project/report/ini_summary.log  
Expected exact content (four lines, Unix LF line endings):

max_connections=200
thread_pool_size=32
enable_caching=true
TOTAL_SECTIONS=3
name: task_000000_820b685c
